question_text: Let 𝐴 be an 𝑛× 𝑛⁡ real matrix. Which of the following statements is/are true?

options.A: If 𝐴 is a symmetric matrix such that 𝐴+ 𝜖⁡𝐼𝑛 is positive semi-definite for every 𝜖> 0, where 𝐼𝑛 is the 𝑛× 𝑛 identity matrix, then 𝐴 is positive semi-definite
options.B: If 𝑛 is odd, then 𝐴−𝐴𝑇 is not invertible
options.C: If 𝐴 is a symmetric matrix such that the singular values of 𝐴 are all strictly positive, then 𝐴 is positive definite
options.D: If 1 is the only singular value of 𝐴, then 𝐴 is orthogonal
